content_id:
Bagian-bagian shalat al-Buhuti dalam Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat mencakup seluruh ranah hukum salah Hanbali dengan ketuntasan sistematis yang menjadi ciri khas kedua syarahnya yang utama. Teks ini melengkapi Kashshaf al-Qina' sekaligus terkadang menyajikan diskusi tambahan atau rumusan alternatif atas posisi-posisi mazhab.

Unsur-unsur wajib shalat dicantumkan dalam Sharh Muntaha dengan empat belas unsur yang sama yang diidentifikasi dalam Kashshaf al-Qina', termasuk persyaratan khas Hanbali tentang shalawat atas keluarga Nabi (formula Ibrahim) dalam tasyahud akhir. Al-Buhuti mendiskusikan redaksi tasyahud yang spesifik dan dua bentuk shalawat Ibrahim yang diajarkan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), mencatat preferensi mazhab Hanbali untuk bentuk yang lengkap.

Bagian-bagian tentang sujud dalam Sharh Muntaha membahas persyaratan Hanbali untuk sajdah yang sah: dahi harus diletakkan di permukaan shalat tanpa penghalang di antara keduanya (kecuali pinggiran sorban yang secara alami berada di tempatnya), hidung harus menyentuh permukaan, dan orang yang shalat harus memberatkan dahinya dan bukan sekadar menyentuhnya. Al-Buhuti mendiskusikan perlakuan terhadap disabilitas yang menghalangi sujud normal, termasuk sujud di kursi atau permukaan yang ditinggikan bagi yang tidak dapat bersujud di lantai.

Bagian-bagian shalat dalam perjalanan pada Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat membahas syarat-syarat untuk meng-qashar dan menjama' shalat. Mazhab Hanbali mensyaratkan jarak perjalanan minimum agar qashar diperbolehkan, yang secara tradisional dihitung sekitar 80 km. Al-Buhuti menyajikan posisi-posisi Hanbali tentang: ambang jarak perjalanan, kondisi di mana musafir dianggap bermukim (dan karenanya tidak dapat lagi meng-qashar shalat), dan izin Hanbali untuk menjama' shalat selama hujan yang sesungguhnya yang menyulitkan para jemaah dalam perjalanan ke masjid.

Shalat Ied mendapatkan pembahasan terperinci dalam Sharh Muntaha. Mazhab Hanbali berpendapat bahwa dua shalat Ied adalah wajib bagi laki-laki Muslim dewasa yang merdeka, bukan sekadar sunnah sebagaimana dipegang oleh mazhab lain. Al-Buhuti menyajikan dasar dalilnya, termasuk perintah Nabi untuk membawa perempuan dan anak-anak ke shalat Ied, beserta syarat-syarat keabsahan shalat.

Shalat jenazah (salat al-janazah) dibahas secara komprehensif dalam Sharh Muntaha. Al-Buhuti mencakup unsur-unsur wajib (empat takbir, bacaan al-Fatihah setelah yang pertama, shalawat atas Nabi setelah yang kedua, doa untuk almarhum setelah yang ketiga, dan taslim setelah yang keempat), doa-doa yang dianjurkan, perlakuan terhadap beberapa jenazah, dan posisi Hanbali tentang menyalatkan mereka yang meninggal dalam keadaan kompromi spiritual.

Bagian-bagian shalat dalam Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat, yang digunakan bersama Kashshaf al-Qina', memberikan ulama dan murid Hanbali rujukan paling lengkap yang tersedia untuk hukum salah mazhab, sebuah rujukan yang tetap aktif di institusi pendidikan Islam tertinggi Arab Saudi.
content_en:
Al-Buhuti's prayer sections in Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat cover the full range of Hanbali salah law with the systematic thoroughness that characterizes both his major commentaries. The text complements Kashshaf al-Qina while sometimes presenting additional discussions or alternative formulations of the school's positions.

The obligatory elements of prayer are listed in Sharh Muntaha with the same fourteen elements identified in Kashshaf al-Qina, including the distinctively Hanbali requirement of prayers upon the Prophet's family (the Ibrahim formula) in the final tashahhud. Al-Buhuti discusses the specific wording of the tashahhud and the two forms of the Ibrahim prayer that the Prophet (peace be upon him) taught, noting the Hanbali school's preference for the complete form.

The sections on prostration in Sharh Muntaha address the Hanbali requirements for valid sajdah: the forehead must be placed on the prayer surface without a intervening barrier (except for a turban edge naturally in place), the nose must touch the surface, and the worshipper must place weight on the forehead rather than merely touching it. Al-Buhuti discusses the treatment of disabilities that prevent normal prostration: the person who cannot prostrate on the ground may prostrate on a chair or elevated surface, while one completely unable to prostrate may make a gesture with the head.

The travel prayer sections of Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat address the conditions for shortening (qasr) and combining (jam') prayers. The Hanbali school requires a minimum journey distance for qasr to be permitted — traditionally calculated as approximately 80 km. Al-Buhuti presents the Hanbali positions on: the journey distance threshold, the conditions under which a traveler is considered stationary (and thus may no longer shorten prayers), and the Hanbali permission for combining prayers during actual rain that inconveniences worshippers traveling to the mosque.

The Eid prayers receive detailed treatment in Sharh Muntaha. The Hanbali school holds that the two Eid prayers are wajib (obligatory) for free adult Muslim males, not merely sunnah as other schools hold. Al-Buhuti presents the evidential basis — including the prophetic command to bring women and children to the Eid prayer — and the conditions for the prayer's validity, including the minimum number required and the correct time (after sunrise has risen the height of a spear).

The funeral prayer (salah al-janazah) is addressed comprehensively in Sharh Muntaha. Al-Buhuti covers the obligatory elements (four takbirs, recitation of al-Fatiha after the first, prayers upon the Prophet after the second, dua for the deceased after the third, and taslim after the fourth), the recommended du'as, the treatment of multiple funerals, and the Hanbali positions on praying over those who died in states of spiritual compromise (those who committed major sins, those who died by suicide).

The prayer sections of Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat, used alongside Kashshaf al-Qina, give Hanbali scholars and students the most complete available reference for the school's salah law — a reference that remains active in Saudi Arabia's highest Islamic educational institutions.
content_ar: Al-Buhuti's الصلاة sections in Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat cover the full range of الحنبلي الصلاة law with the systematic thoroughness that characterizes both his major commentaries. The text complements Kashshaf al-Qina بينما sometimes presenting additional discussions or alternative formulations of the school's positions. The obligatory elements of الصلاة are listed in Sharh Muntaha with the same fourteen elements identified in Kashshaf al-Qina, بما في ذلك the distinctively الحنبلي requirement of الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ's الأسرة (the إبراهيم ﷺ formula) in the final التشهد. Al-Buhuti discusses the specific wording of the التشهد and the two forms of the إبراهيم ﷺ الصلاة that النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) taught, noting the الحنبلي school's preference for the complete form. The sections on prostration in Sharh Muntaha address the الحنبلي requirements for valid السجدة: the forehead must be placed on the الصلاة surface without a intervening barrier (except for a turban edge naturally in place), the nose must touch the surface, and the worshipper must place weight on the forehead rather than merely touching it. Al-Buhuti discusses the treatment of disabilities that prevent normal prostration: the person who cannot prostrate on the ground may prostrate on a chair or elevated surface, بينما one completely unable to prostrate may make a gesture with the head. The travel الصلاة sections of Sharh Muntaha al-Iradat address the conditions for shortening (qasr) and combining (jam') الصلوات. The الحنبلي school requires a minimum journey distance for qasr to be permitted — traditionally calculated as approximately 80 km. Al-Buhuti presents the الحنبلي positions on: the journey distance threshold, the conditions under which a traveler is considered stationary (and وهكذا may no longer shorten الصلوات), and the الحنبلي permission for combining الصلوات خلال actual rain that inconveniences worshippers traveling to the المسجد. The Eid الصلوات receive detailed treatment in Sharh Muntaha. The الحنبلي school holds that the two Eid الصلوات are الواجب (obligatory) for free adult مسلم males, not merely السنة as other schools hold. Al-Buhuti presents the evidential basis — بما في ذلك the prophetic command to bring women and الأولاد to the Eid الصلاة — and the conditions for the الصلاة's validity, بما في ذلك the minimum number required and the correct time (بعد sunrise has risen the height of a spear). The funeral الصلاة (الصلاة al-janazah) is addressed comprehensively in Sharh Muntaha. Al-Buhuti covers the obligatory elements (four takbirs, التلاوة of al-Fatiha بعد the أولاً, الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ بعد the ثانياً, الدعاء for the deceased بعد the ثالثاً, and التسليم بعد the رابعاً), the recommended du'as, the treatment of multiple funerals, and the الحنبلي positions on praying over those who died in states of spiritual compromise (those who committed major الذنوب, those who died by suicide).
